summary: Group favourites on pedigree and individual quality under Rudi Garcia, but recent tournaments have disappointed (group exit in 2022, Round of 16 at Euro 2024). A 4-2-3-1 blending exciting young talent with an experienced core (De Bruyne, Courtois, Lukaku) — devastating going forward, but vulnerable in transition and at set pieces.
style: 4-2-3-1 — the winger/full-back relationship is everything: full-backs (Meunier, De Cuyper, Castagne) make varied off-ball runs (overlaps to isolate Doku 1v1, underlaps for a goal threat) to free roaming wingers Doku, Saelemaekers and Trossard. Centre-forwards (De Ketelaere, Openda) drop deep to pull defenders and let the wingers and De Bruyne run beyond. Build from the back with a midfielder dropping into the full-back zone.

counterReliance.note: Free-flowing attack with pace in behind; exposed in transition

strengths: Elite 1v1 wingers with coordinated overlapping/underlapping support
Pace and quality in behind, plus an experienced spine (De Bruyne, Courtois)
Prolific — 39 goals in Garcia's first 12 games
weaknesses: Vulnerable defending transitions when they lose the ball
Disjointed high press (4-4-2) leaves spaces when not pressing in unison
Poor set-piece defending — conceded from corners vs Mexico, USA and Wales (tracking/communication); shipped 3 to Ukraine and 5 across two games to Wales

keyPlayers.name: Jérémy Doku
keyPlayers.note: 9.0 take-ons per 90 — 2nd only to Yamal in Europe's top-five leagues; now more productive (2.98 chances created/90, top for key passes after a dribble); 5 goals, 4 assists in 11 under Garcia

keyPlayers.name: Kevin De Bruyne
keyPlayers.note: The experienced creative core — drops to build, then bursts beyond the dropping forwards

tactics: Free the wingers with full-back runs and drop the strikers to open space for runners; build patiently from the back. The talent is top-tier, but the games can swing on their transition defending and set-piece frailty.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group G (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
